operating_state: Net sales increased 32.7% to $37.5 billion driven by 31.0% comparable sales growth, reflecting 19.1% increase in customer transactions and 10.3% increase in average ticket. All merchandising departments posted positive comparable sales with 13 of 14 departments achieving growth at or above 20%, led by Lumber and Kitchen and Bath. Online sales represented 14.3% of total sales and grew 27.1%, while inventory turnover improved to 5.5 times from 5.0 times driven by elevated customer demand across categories.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin compressed slightly to 34.0% from 34.1% due to unfavorable product mix, offset by reduced promotional markdowns in the strong demand environment. SG&A expenses decreased as percentage of sales to 17.0% from 20.6%, benefiting from sales leverage and cycling $864 million in prior-year COVID-19 expenses, partially offset by higher hourly payroll costs and increased bonus accruals from outperformance.
material_events: Resumed share repurchases in Q1 with $3.8 billion in buybacks after suspension during COVID-19 pandemic. Announced 10% quarterly dividend increase to $1.65 per share in February 2021. Fully repaid $1.35 billion in 2.00% senior notes due April 2021 in March. Board approved new $20.0 billion share repurchase authorization in May 2021, replacing prior $15.0 billion program.
macro_exposures: Commodity price inflation, particularly in lumber, contributed to average ticket growth but pressured gross margins through unfavorable product mix. Weaker U.S. dollar provided $157 million positive impact to sales growth. No material changes to risk factors from prior period disclosures.
forward_outlook: Management estimates annual capital expenditures of approximately 2% of net sales while maintaining disciplined capital allocation approach. Company expects to maintain elevated cash position during fiscal 2021 as circumstances warrant, with flexibility to adjust capital expenditures based on business operations or economic environment.
uncertainty_profile: Received unsolicited mini-tender offer from Tutanota LLC for 1,000,000 shares at $360.00 per share, which company does not endorse and recommends shareholders reject. No material changes to critical accounting policies or pending accounting standard impacts during the quarter.
